action.reasoning: Observation: All recent heuristic planners (frontier-based) achieve 0% success in local tests; robot explores poorly. The best known score (0.5502) came from a simple utility-greedy + BFS heuristic in iteration 13, but subsequent attempts have been buggy. Analysis: The frontier approach has bugs (coordinate mapping, frontier selection). Reverting to the proven greedy utility+BFS algorithm should recover success rate > 0.8 and score ~0.55. Need to write a clean, robust implementation, test locally on 9 training maps to verify success > 0, then submit evaluation. Decision: write predict.py with UtilityGreedyPlanner (eps=0.0, short tabu). Run test_policy.py to confirm, then submit eval in next iteration if test passes. Use remaining iterations to refine path efficiency (e.g., distance-weighted selection, A*).
